from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent English sentence.  The function now builds relative clauses when an
    object appears as a subject elsewhere, producing more natural complex sentences.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping predicates to natural‑language phrases.
    _phrase_map = {
        "almaMater": "graduated from",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was originally named",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired in",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "was",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "participated in",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "is a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as Chief of the Astronaut Office in",
        "title": "held the title of",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon award",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "had crew members",
        "representative": "had representative",
        "alternativeName": "also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senators",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    # Organise triples by subject for quick lookup
    subj_dict = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj_dict.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # Choose the first subject as the main focus
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    main_triples = subj_dict.get(main_subject, [])

    clauses = []
    for t in main_triples:
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = t.object.strip('"')
        phrase = _phrase_map.get(pred, None)

        # Base clause for the main subject
        if phrase:
            # add unit for timeInSpace if missing
            if pred == "timeInSpace" and not any(u in obj.lower() for u in ["minute", "hour", "day"]):
                obj = f"{obj} minutes"
            base = f"{main_subject} {phrase} {obj}"
        else:
            base = f"{main_subject} {pred} {obj}"

        # If the object is also a subject elsewhere, build a relative clause
        if obj in subj_dict:
            rel_parts = []
            for rel_t in subj_dict[obj]:
                rel_pred = rel_t.predicate
                rel_obj = rel_t.object.strip('"')
                rel_phrase = _phrase_map.get(rel_pred, None)
                if rel_phrase:
                    # special handling for timeInSpace inside relative clause
                    if rel_pred == "timeInSpace" and not any(u in rel_obj.lower() for u in ["minute", "hour", "day"]):
                        rel_obj = f"{rel_obj} minutes"
                    rel_parts.append(f"{rel_phrase} {rel_obj}")
                else:
                    rel_parts.append(f"{rel_pred} {rel_obj}")
            # join relative parts
            rel_clause = " and ".join(rel_parts)
            base = f"{base}, which {rel_clause}"
        clauses.append(base)

    # Combine all clauses into one complex sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
